title: senecio squalidus subsp. calabricus
intro: Senecio squalidus subsp. calabricus, also known as the old man's thistle or Jacob's ladder, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Senecio squalidus subsp. calabricus include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Senecio squalidus subsp. calabricus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
